tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58318,"title":"[Batch] Docs: Fix broken NuGet link, malformed Next Steps, and sparse GA changelog in Azure.Compute.Batch README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Compute.Batch` **Service directory:** `sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #56264) by `@wiboris` ## What Changed PR #56264 promoted `Azure.Compute.Batch` to GA (1.0.0). It updated the README, CHANGELOG, and MigrationGuide as part of the release, but introduced or left several documentation issues. ## Gaps Found - **Broken NuGet package link** in the README header badge row: the link reads `(www.nuget.org/redacted) with no package path, so it lands on the NuGet homepage rather than the `Azure.Compute.Batch` package page - **Malformed "Next steps" section**: the section body reads `View more https://github.com/.../samples here for common usages ... [Batch Samples]((redacted))` — a raw URL appears inline in the text alongside a separate hyperlink, making the sentence awkward and redundant - **Sparse GA CHANGELOG entry**: the `1.0.0 (2026-03-01)` section contains only "GA release, see migration guide if migrating from Microsoft.Azure.Batch" — it should include a brief summary of what changed from 1.0.0-beta.5 to GA so users can understand the release at a glance 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/README.md` — Edit **Fix 1 — NuGet link** (near top of file, in the badge/links row): Replace: ```markdown [Package (NuGet)]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` With: ```markdown [Package (NuGet)]((www.nuget.org/redacted) ``` **Fix 2 — Next Steps section** (near the bottom of the file): Replace: ```markdown ## Next steps View more https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/samples here for common usages of the Batch client library: [Batch Samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/samples). ``` With: ```markdown ## Next steps View more common usages of the Batch client library in the [Batch Samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/samples) folder. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/CHANGELOG.md` — Edit Replace the sparse GA entry: ```markdown ## 1.0.0 (2026-03-01) ### Other Changes GA release, see migration guide if migrating from Microsoft.Azure.Batch ``` With a richer summary that reflects what this release represents: ```markdown ## 1.0.0 (2026-03-01) ### Features Added - General Availability (GA) release of `Azure.Compute.Batch`, the next-generation Azure Batch client library for .NET - Full support for all Batch service operations: pools, jobs, job schedules, tasks, and nodes - Long-running operation support via `WaitUntil` for operations including `DeleteJob`, `DeletePool`, `ResizePool`, `RebootNode`, `ReimageNode`, `StartNode`, `DeallocateNode`, and others - Microsoft Entra ID and Shared Key authentication support ### Other Changes - See the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/MigrationGuide.md) for guidance on migrating from `Microsoft.Azure.Batch` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet regions are required for these fixes. The existing `#region Snippet:` tags in the test sample files are already wired up correctly. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps (needs the text fix above) 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed beyond the two targeted fixes above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 batch` — confirm README is updated correctly via `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 batch` — only needed if any public API was changed (not expected for these doc fixes) 5. `dotnet format sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24588272514/agentic_workflow) · ● 451.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58318","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T21:59:42Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T21:59:42Z"}

General Availability (GA) release of `Azure.Compute.Batch`, the next-generation Azure Batch client library for .NET - Full support for all Batch service operations: pools, jobs, job schedules, tasks, and nodes - Long-running operation support via `WaitUntil` for operations including `DeleteJob`, `DeletePool`, `ResizePool`, `RebootNode`, `ReimageNode`, `StartNode`, `DeallocateNode`, and others - Microsoft Entra ID and Shared Key authentication support ### Other Changes - See the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/MigrationGuide.md) for guidance on migrating from `Microsoft.Azure.Batch` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet regions are required for these fixes. The existing `#region Snippet:` tags in the test sample files are already wired up correctly. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps (needs the text fix above) 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed beyond the two targeted fixes above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 batch` — confirm README is updated correctly via `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 batch` — only needed if any public API was changed (not expected for these doc fixes) 5. `dotnet format sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24588272514/agentic_workflow) · ● 451.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58318","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T21:59:42Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T21:59:42Z"}

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3493286297,"number":53058,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"[BUG] When creating a BatchTaskCreateOptions, there is no way to specify ResourceFiles,  ApplicationPackageReferences, EnvironmentSettings, etc","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Compute.Batch 1.0.0-beta3

### Describe the bug

I want to be able to specify all the properties of the BatchTaskCreateOptions. There is only a simply constructor that takes two parameters. The complete constructor is internal so can't call it. The properties like ResourceFiles,  ApplicationPackageReferences, EnvironmentSettings, etc are read only so can't be set.

### Expected behavior

I should be able to change all the properties in a BatchTaskCreateOptions.

### Actual behavior

There is only a simply constructor that takes two parameters. The complete constructor is internal so can't call it. The properties like ResourceFiles,  ApplicationPackageReferences, EnvironmentSettings, etc are read only so can't be set.

### Reproduction Steps

Create a BatchTaskCreateOptions, specify the ResourceFiles,  ApplicationPackageReferences, EnvironmentSettings.

### Environment

Visual Studio 2022. .Net SDK 9.0. .Net Framework 4.8.1. Windows 11 Pro 24H2.","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"junma25","id":195407842,"node_id":"U_kgDOC6Wv4g","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/195407842?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/junma25","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/junma25/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":185011561,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Batch","name":"Batch","color":"e99695","description":"","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODUwMTE1NjE="},{"id":1178447860,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client","name":"Client","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a non-management package","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ3ODYw"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz"},{"id":1369687945,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question","name":"question","color":"eaa875","description":"The issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved. that","default":true,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzY5Njg3OTQ1"},{"id":3059080257,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/issue-addressed","name":"issue-addressed","color":"5df772","description":"Workflow: The Azure SDK team believes it to be addressed and ready to close.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzMDU5MDgwMjU3"}],"assignee":{"login":"jsquire","id":913445,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjkxMzQ0NQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/jsquire","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/subscriptions"},"comments":4,"closed_at":"2025-10-15T16:39:22Z","created_at":"2025-10-07T23:04:05Z","updated_at":"2026-01-13T17:42:00Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53058","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53058","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53058/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53058/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53058/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53058/reactions"},"assignees":[{"login":"jsquire","id":913445,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjkxMzQ0NQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/jsquire","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jsquire/subscriptions"}],"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM7QN1GZ"},{"id":914938270,"number":21690,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"[FEATURE REQ] Make batchClient Mockable","body":"**Library or service name.**
What library or service is this request related to? [e.g. Azure.Storage.Blobs]
[Azure.Batch.BatchClient](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/batch/Microsoft.Azure.Batch/src/BatchClient.cs#L56)
 
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
What feature would you like to get added? What problem is it solving?
Currently it is not possible to Mock a BatchClient using Moq due to batchClient having no  Interface or public constructor.
Would it be possible to make the private constructor public, or add an interface for this.

Example of error when mocking batchClient:

```csharp
_mockBatchClient = new Mock<BatchClient>();
_mockBatchClientFactory
                    .Setup(_ => _.CreateBatchClient(It.IsAny<string>(), It.IsAny<string>(), It.IsAny<string>()))
                    .Returns(_mockBatchClient.Object);
```
 
 
This results in the error previously mentioned:
Castle.DynamicProxy.InvalidProxyConstructorArgumentsException : Can not instantiate proxy of class: Microsoft.Azure.Batch.BatchClient. Could not find a parameterless constructor.

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies three documentation gaps in `Azure.Compute.Batch` introduced with the GA (1.0.0) release: a broken NuGet badge link in the README, a malformed "Next steps" section, and a sparse CHANGELOG entry.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Compute.Batch` (1.0.0 GA)
**Affected API:** Documentation files — `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`
**Scenarios:**
- Broken NuGet package link in the README badge row lands on NuGet homepage instead of the `Azure.Compute.Batch` package page
- "Next steps" section contains a raw URL inline alongside a separate hyperlink, making the text redundant and awkward
- The `1.0.0 (2026-03-01)` CHANGELOG entry is sparse — only says "GA release" with no summary of changes from 1.0.0-beta.5

**Root ask:** Fix the three documentation issues in `sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/README.md` and `sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/CHANGELOG.md` as detailed in the issue implementation guide.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue, not a runtime bug. The gaps were introduced or left unaddressed during the GA promotion PR #56264.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Inspect `sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/README.md` badge row for the broken NuGet link and the "Next steps" section for the redundant URL
2. Review `sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/CHANGELOG.md` `1.0.0` entry and compare against the beta.5 changelog to enumerate what changed
3. Apply the targeted fixes from the implementation guide provided in the issue body
4. Run `dotnet build sdk/batch/Azure.Compute.Batch/` and `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 batch` to confirm no regressions
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.Compute.Batch` is a data-plane client SDK (not a management `Azure.ResourceManager.*` package and not a provisioning library)
- **Service:** `Batch` — the issue explicitly names `Azure.Compute.Batch` in the `sdk/batch/` directory
- **Confidence:** High — the package name, service directory, and reference issues (e.g., #53058) all confirm `Client` + `Batch`; no ambiguity between services or categories
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Batch` (line 139) — the only `%Batch` ServiceLabel entry; matched because the issue carries the `Batch` service label
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @dpwatrous @wiboris
- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` label; issue left unassigned (only ServiceOwners, no AzureSdkOwners); `needs-team-attention` not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** No multi-label entries for `%Batch` exist in CODEOWNERS; the single `%Batch` entry at line 139 was the only candidate and matched immediately
</details>
